![SotaTek][image1]

*SYSTEM & SMART CONTRACT OVERVIEW*

**EXBOT**

Delta-neutral Uniswap V3 liquidity, hedged on Hyperliquid

Version: v1.0

Authored by: Daniel

June 2026

[**8\. Why It Stands Out	10**](#heading=)

# **1\. What ExBot Is**

ExBot is an automated investment bot. An investor deposits **USDC**, and ExBot puts that capital to work across **two venues** at once:

* **A Uniswap V3 liquidity position** (on-chain) that earns trading fees.

* **A Hyperliquid portion** (off-chain) that runs a **short hedge** to offset the price exposure of the LP position.

Providing liquidity on Uniswap earns fees, but it also exposes the investor to **impermanent loss** — when ETH moves, the LP position drifts in value. The Hyperliquid short cancels most of that price exposure, so the strategy is broadly **delta-neutral**: the investor keeps earning Uniswap fees while the hedge absorbs the up-and-down of ETH.

A key convenience: **the investor only ever deals in USDC.** They never set up a Hyperliquid account, never approve a delegate, and never touch the hedge directly — the platform's Operator handles the Hyperliquid side on their behalf.

![ExBot flow diagram][image2]

# **2\. How It Works**

## **Two legs working together**

**The Uniswap leg (on-chain).** A portion of the deposited USDC is swapped and placed into a concentrated-liquidity position within a chosen price range. While the price stays in range, it earns trading fees. If the price drifts to the edge, ExBot **rebalances** — re-centering the position so it keeps earning.

**The Hyperliquid leg (off-chain).** The remaining portion is allocated to a **short hedge** on Hyperliquid, sized to the LP's ETH exposure. As the LP exposure changes, the Operator adjusts the hedge so the combined position stays balanced. These funds are routed through an Operator-controlled **EXBOT Agent Wallet** — not the investor's wallet, and not Hyperliquid's user-delegation feature.

## **Two ways to exit**

| Action | When | Effect |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Withdraw** | Capital is still **unspent** (not yet deployed by the strategy) | Returns the idle USDC to the investor |
| **Redeem** | Capital is **deployed** (positions exist) | Liquidates **everything** — the Uniswap LP and the Hyperliquid portion |

## **Always supervised**

The Operator continuously monitors both legs — the LP's price range and the hedge's health — and only acts when something needs to change (rebalance the LP, adjust the hedge, or trigger emergency controls).

# **3\. System Components**

A key architectural point: **ExBot runs as its own standalone Cloudflare Worker, separate from the Operator.** All of ExBot's strategy logic and scheduling live *inside the ExBot Worker*, which runs autonomously on its own cron. The Operator is only a thin, public-facing entry point that forwards three actions — **start / status / close** — into the ExBot Worker. Everything else (monitoring, rebalancing, hedging, reconciliation) happens inside the ExBot Worker without the Operator's involvement.

![ExBot flow diagram][image3]

Top-level building blocks:

| Component | Role |
| :---- | :---- |
| **ExBot Worker** *(standalone)* | The autonomous engine. Runs everything end-to-end on its own schedule: cron-driven scans, the queue/consumer pipeline, Durable Objects (per-user lock, rate-limit, market-data cache), the Strategy & Math Engine, the Hyperliquid hedge adapter, the **on-chain relayer that signs Vault transactions**, and its own D1 database. **All ExBot logic lives here.** |
| **Operator API** *(facade)* | A separate, public-facing service. Exposes only start / status / close and forwards them to the ExBot Worker. It holds **none** of ExBot's strategy logic and does not drive the monitoring loop. |
| **Smart Contract Suite** | On-chain. Custodies USDC, holds the LP position, executes strategy logic, routes fees, and tracks pending redemptions. See Section 5 for the seven contracts. |
| **POOL UI** | Where the investor deposits, reviews position health and PnL, and withdraws or redeems. |
| **Backtesting Suite** | Offline. Crawls historical market data and replays it through the **same** Strategy & Math Engine used live, so any configuration can be evaluated on real past data before going live. |

The Strategy & Math Engine and the Hyperliquid hedge adapter are modules *inside* the ExBot Worker. The math engine is a pure decision core (sizing, hedge target, impermanent-loss math, triggers) that is shared, unchanged, with the Backtesting Suite — what you test is what runs live.

# **4\. Default Parameters (Phase A)**

ExBot launches with a fixed, conservative parameter set. These are the defaults the strategy runs on today:

| Parameter | Default | Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **LP price range** | **±5%** | Single-tier band around the current price (range ladder deferred to a later phase). |
| **Hedge ratio** | **70%** | “Balanced” — the short covers \~70% of the LP's ETH exposure. |
| **Hedge leverage** | **3×** | Margin for the short \= target short notional ÷ 3\. |
| **Positions per user** | **1 active bot** | One user \= one active position at a time. |
| **Pool** | **WETH–USDC** | Primary LP pool, on Base / Optimism. |
| **Performance fee** | **30% of gross PnL** | Paid in USDC to the performance-fee recipient(s). |
| **Operation fee** | **BNZA buyback & burn** | A share is used to buy back BNZA and burn it. |

These are platform-level defaults for the current phase. Configurable per-investor parameters (e.g. range width, hedge ratio) can be opened up in later phases; the backtesting suite already supports sweeping them.

# **5\. Smart Contracts (On-Chain Layer)**

The on-chain suite handles **USDC custody, the Uniswap leg, fee routing, and redemption tracking**. The Hyperliquid leg lives off-chain by design. There are **seven** ExBot contracts:

| \# | Contract | Role |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 1 | **BnzaExVault** | The core contract. Custodies investor USDC, is the entry point for deposit / withdraw / redeem, validates strategies against an allowlist, and dispatches execution to the right strategy contract. Exposes emergency controls. |
| 2 | **OpenPositionStrategyV1** | Stateless. Opens a position: BNZA buyback, swap, and mint the Uniswap LP. |
| 3 | **ClosePosStrategyV1** | Stateless. Handles redeem: liquidates the Uniswap LP and queues the Hyperliquid-portion release. |
| 4 | **RebalanceStrategyV1** | Stateless. Re-centers the LP range when price moves out of bounds. |
| 5 | **BnzaExPositionManager** | Receives and **holds the LP NFT** from Uniswap, and is the registry of which investor owns which position. |
| 6 | **TokenRouter** | Routes fee proceeds: USDC to the performance-fee recipients, BNZA to buyback-and-burn. |
| 7 | **RedemptionQueue** | Tracks **pending Hyperliquid-portion redemptions** awaiting Operator fulfillment. |

Strategy contracts are **stateless** and added via an allowlist — new strategies can be introduced without changing the core contracts.

## **Key functions on the Vault**

* **Investor:** deposit(usdc) · withdraw(usdc) *(unspent capital only)* · redeem(tokenId) *(liquidate all positions)*

* **Operator:** executeStrategy(...) · vaultRebalance(...) · vaultClose(...) · collectFees(...)

* **Emergency (multi-sig):** emergencyTransfer(...) · pause / unpause · rotateOperator(...)

## **External infrastructure (third-party)**

* **Uniswap V3** — NonfungiblePositionManager (mint / decrease / collect), V3SwapRouter (swaps), V3Factory, and the WETH-USDC and BNZA-USDC pools.

* **Hyperliquid** — the off-chain venue for the hedge portion (reached via the Operator's Agent Wallet).

## **Custody & security model**

* **USDC custody** — the vault holds deposited USDC until the Operator deploys it. Idle (unspent) funds can be reclaimed any time via withdraw.

* **LP NFT protection** — the position NFT is held by BnzaExPositionManager; investors can't accidentally move or revoke it.

* **Separated authority** — investor, Operator, and emergency multi-sig powers are strictly partitioned. Only the Operator can deploy funds; only the multi-sig can pause or rotate the operator.

* **Emergency governance** — a **multi-signature** role (not a single key) can transfer a position out or pause the contract in a crisis.

* **Standard safeguards** — reentrancy guards, slippage limits on every swap, and namespaced (ERC-7201) storage for safe upgrades.

## **Fees**

| Fee | Amount | Destination |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Performance fee** | 30% of gross PnL | USDC → performance-fee recipient wallet(s) |
| **Operation fee** | — | BNZA **buyback & burn** (sent to 0x0000) |

The remainder of the proceeds goes back to the investor.

# **6\. End-to-End Flows**

In every flow below, the **Operator API only relays start / close** to the ExBot Worker. All execution, signing, and scheduling is done by the **ExBot Worker**.

## **6.1 Deposit & open**

![ExBot flow diagram][image4]

The investor deposits USDC and clicks start; the Operator facade simply forwards that to the ExBot Worker. The ExBot Worker runs safety checks, opens the Uniswap LP on-chain (NFT held in custody), allocates the Hyperliquid short off-chain, and begins its own cron-driven monitoring. **No Hyperliquid setup or approval is required from the investor.**

## **6.2 Redeem (two-phase)**

![ExBot flow diagram][image5]

A redeem liquidates everything. The **Uniswap leg settles instantly** in the same transaction; the **Hyperliquid leg is released by the ExBot Worker** shortly after and recorded in the RedemptionQueue. If the Uniswap step fails, the whole transaction reverts and nothing changes.

## **6.3 Rebalance (autonomous — no Operator involvement)**

![ExBot flow diagram][image6]

This loop is driven entirely by the **ExBot Worker's own cron** — the Operator is not involved. When a scan detects the price leaving the LP's range, the Worker re-centers the position on-chain and re-syncs the Hyperliquid hedge to the new exposure. The same autonomous loop handles hedge adjustments, margin checks, and reconciliation.

# **7\. Custody & Trust Model**

ExBot is a **managed, custodial service** — not a non-custodial or trustless protocol. Once capital is deployed, the system holds and operates the positions on the investor's behalf. This is what makes the automation possible, but it's important the investor understands exactly what that means.

**The investor does not directly manage the positions.** Their on-chain actions are limited to deposit, withdraw (unspent capital only), and redeem (liquidate everything). They cannot manually adjust the LP range, the hedge, or move the position.

The degree of control differs between the two legs:

|  | Uniswap leg (on-chain) | Hyperliquid leg (off-chain) |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Where funds sit** | BnzaExVault / LP NFT in BnzaExPositionManager | EXBOT **Agent Wallet** (operator-controlled) |
| **Who can operate it** | Operator only, **and only via allowlisted strategy code** | Operator (the HL account is the operator's) |
| **Exit guarantee** | **Enforced on-chain** — redeem returns LP value in the same transaction; multi-sig can rescue the NFT to the investor | **No on-chain guarantee** — return depends on the operator fulfilling the request |
| **RedemptionQueue role** | n/a | A **tracker/notifier, not an escrow** — it records the pending request; it does not hold or guarantee the funds |

**What protects the investor on the Uniswap side:** the Operator can only act through fixed, allowlisted strategy contracts (it cannot send funds to arbitrary addresses), the investor can always force an exit via redeem, and an independent multi-signature role can recover the NFT in an emergency.

**The trust assumption on the Hyperliquid side:** the hedge funds live in an operator-controlled wallet with no on-chain claim for the investor. Returning the investor's Hyperliquid share relies on the operator executing the redemption — an **operational/trust guarantee, not a smart-contract one.**

**Where risk concentrates:** the security of the strategy ultimately rests on the **operator/relayer key** and the **EXBOT Agent Wallet**. These are protected by envelope encryption (Cloudflare Secrets Store) and multi-sig emergency controls, but they remain the central trust points — on-chain safeguards fully cover the Uniswap leg, while the Hyperliquid leg depends on sound operation.

# **8\. Why It Stands Out**

* **Earn while protected** — collect Uniswap trading fees while a managed short hedge neutralizes most ETH price risk.

* **USDC in, USDC out** — the investor deals only in USDC and never has to touch Hyperliquid; the platform handles the hedge.

* **Fully automated, always supervised** — opening, rebalancing, hedging, and closing are handled end-to-end, with continuous monitoring.

* **Test before you trust** — any configuration can be backtested on real historical data using the identical decision engine that runs live.

* **Custody with guardrails** — operator restrictions, multi-signature emergency controls, NFT custody, and atomic on-chain settlement.
